summary: Iteration 88: Mobile CSS Migration Testing - ALL TESTS PASSED. Verified complete mobile CSS consolidation (11 legacy files → single mobile.css ~190 lines). Desktop completely unaffected. Mobile responsive behaviors working correctly across all 7 homepage sections.

tests: All 7 sections present: hero, intelligence-diagram, stats, carousel, cognition, cta, footer
Nav shows all links: Platform, Intelligence, Integrations, Pricing, Blog, Trust
Nav login and get-started buttons visible
Page scrolls normally, footer visible after scroll
BIQc core node centered (offset: 0.008px from center)
Cognition cards stack single column (6 cards)
Desktop nav links hidden on mobile
Mobile nav height: 56px with hamburger menu

critical_code_review_comments: Mobile CSS consolidation successful - single source of truth for responsive styles
Desktop styles completely unaffected by mobile.css (all rules scoped to @media max-width:768px)
Scroll fundamentals properly global (html/body overflow rules outside media query)
All data-testid selectors correctly targeted for mobile overrides
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile CSS migration complete and verified. Single mobile.css file (~190 lines) replaces 11 legacy files (3138 lines). Desktop unchanged. Key mobile behaviors: canvas hidden, arrows hidden, h1=24px, CTA full-width, grids collapse to single/2-column, page scrolls normally. All 7 homepage sections render correctly in both viewports.

mocked_api_confirmation.note: Homepage is static marketing page - no API calls required
